channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Thelma Mothershed Wair, a member of the Little Rock Nine who integrated an Arkansas school, has died
item.description: Thelma Mothershed Wair, one of the nine Black students who integrated a high school in Arkansas' capital city of Little Rock in 1957 while a mob of white segregationists yelled threats and insults, has died at age 83.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: President Biden and first lady toast a reimagined White House tour that she's unveiling on Monday
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden and first lady Jill Biden on Sunday toasted the unveiling of a long-awaited reimagined public White House tour. The couple held a dinner in celebration of the first lady's update of the White House public tour. The updates will be unveiled on Monday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2 Navy aviators are declared dead after a fighter jet crashed in Washington state
item.description: Two crew members who were missing following the crash of a fighter jet in mountainous terrain in Washington state during a routine training flight have been declared dead, the U.S. Navy said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Asheville woman discovers home burglarized after fleeing hurricane
item.description: "They took all [my husband's] power tools that were light and a bunch of different equipment," Jacqui Fehl said.
item.dc:creator: Harrison Grubb, NewsNation

item.title: Calmer weather helps contain Oakland, California, fire that forced evacuations
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Firefighting crews took advantage of calmer weather Sunday to increase containment of a small wildfire in Oakland, California, that burned two homes and forced more than 500 people to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nebraska Rep. Don Bacon urges 'respect' for election
item.description: “We have to respect our elections, and I get it, candidates have the right to go to court and appeal, or to challenge versus … various processes,” Bacon told NewsNation’s Chris Stirewalt.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame turns up starpower to induct Cher, Foreigner, Mary J. Blige, Ozzy and more
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Hollywood stars Julia Roberts and Zendaya bookended Saturday’s inductions into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, giving a little stardust to an eclectic lineup that included pop icon Cher, heavy metal’s Ozzy Osbourne, hip-hop soul queen Mary J.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Popular Florida restaurant burns down on day it reopened after hurricane
item.description: A popular restaurant in Cape Coral burned down on the same day it had reopened after Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, ABC 4

item.title: Singer among victims in couple's alleged Las Vegas theft spree during Super Bowl week
item.description: A married couple allegedly stole hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of valuables as Las Vegas hosted the Super Bowl, with one theft involving singer Jason Derulo.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, ABC 4

item.title: Mormon church now offering sleeveless garments for members in warmer climates
item.description: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints is now offering sleeveless garments for members in hot and humid climates, according to a Church spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, KREX 5

item.title: Expelled Indian high commissioner denies involvement in murder of Sikh leader in Canada
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — India’s high commissioner to Canada has denied any involvement in the murder of a Canadian Sikh leader who was killed in British Columbia last year even though the Canadian government has named him as a person of interest in the assassination.
item.dc:creator: JIM MORRIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Orionid meteor shower peaks tonight: How to see it
item.description: The Orionids, which began in late September, are considered the most beautiful of the annual meteor showers.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Evans, NewsNation

item.title: Many gift-buying parents pressured to overspend: Survey
item.description: According to NerdWallet’s annual holiday spending report, Americans are spending quite a bit more on holiday travel and gift shopping than last year.
item.dc:creator: Erin El Issa, NewsNation

item.title: 2 Navy aviators declared dead after fighter jet crash in Washington state
item.description: The aviators' names won’t be released until a day after their next of kin have been notified, the Navy said in a statement Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: One of the last Navajo Code Talkers from World War II dies at 107
item.description: One of the last remaining Navajo Code Talkers who transmitted messages during World War II using the tribe's native language has died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Singer among victims in couple's alleged Las Vegas theft spree during Super Bowl week
item.description: A married couple allegedly stole hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of valuables as Las Vegas hosted the Super Bowl, with one theft involving singer Jason Derulo.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia authorities investigating a dock gangway collapse that killed 7 on a historic island
item.description: Georgia authorities say they are investigating the catastrophic failure of a dock gangway that collapsed, killing seven on Sapelo Island off the state's Atlantic seacoast.
item.dc:creator: Russ Bynum And Emily Wagster Pettus, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In New York, a constitutional amendment provides election fodder for the left and the right
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Democrats pushed to get a constitutional amendment on New York's ballot because they believed it could energize liberals eager to protect abortion rights.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How the 2025 Social Security COLA compares to recent years
item.description: A recent survey from the Motley Fool shows 50% of retirees have considered going back to work to make ends meet.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Grandma who texted stranger for Thanksgiving announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The Arizona grandmother who sent a text message to what she believed was her grandson’s phone number, inviting him over for Thanksgiving dinner nearly nine years ago, has announced her diagnosis of breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Many gift-buying parents pressured to overspend this holiday season: Survey
item.description: (NerdWallet) - Holiday shopping season is upon us, and the pressure is on, or at least it feels that way for some parents.
item.dc:creator: Erin El Issa, Fox 5

item.title: Texas' junk science law is getting another look over Robert Roberson's case
item.description: A man whose execution was halted will testify at a legislative hearing about a “junk science” law in Texas that many say should have saved him long ago.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: From showgirl feathers to shimmering chandeliers, casino kitsch finds new life
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Crystal chandeliers that once glimmered above a swanky lounge, bright blue costume feathers that cloaked shimmying showgirls, and fake palm trees that evoked a desert oasis are just some the artifacts making their way from the latest latest casino graveyards of Las Vegas into Sin Ci
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Olympian to drug lord: Snowboarder accused of running drug ring
item.description: Wedding competed for Canada in the 2002 Winter Olympics in Salt Lake City; according to the official Olympics website, he finished 24th in the parallel giant slalom event.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: Singer named as victim in couple's alleged Las Vegas theft spree
item.description: A married couple allegedly stole hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of valuables as Las Vegas hosted the Super Bowl -- with one theft involving a popular singer, according to documents the 8 News Now Investigators obtained.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: At least 7 people killed after Georgia ferry dock gangway collapse: officials
item.description: Georgia officials are investigating after a freak ferry dock collapse on Sapelo Island killed at least seven people and injured an unknown number of others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah court allows Australian student to play high school football after lawsuit filed
item.description: An international student who filed a discrimination lawsuit earlier this month has been allowed to play on his high school's football team.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, Fox 5

item.title: 2 people shown on camera leaving fatally shot teen at Las Vegas hospital
item.description: North Las Vegas police are asking for the public's help to identify two people they say were caught on camera leaving a fatally shot teen in a hospital lobby.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Oscar brings winds, heavy rains to eastern Cuba after striking the Bahamas
item.description: Hurricane Oscar has made landfall in eastern Cuba, an island beleaguered by a massive power outage, after striking the southeastern Bahamas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Viral sensation 'Lieutenant Dan' arrested for boat being 'public health hazard': police
item.description: A Florida man known as "Lieutenant Dan" who went viral for claiming he was refusing to leave his small home boat ahead of Hurricane Milton has now been arrested for failing to leave a park-side dock.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: It's more dangerous to drive at night in these states than anywhere else in the US
item.description: Driving at night is dangerous no matter where you are in the U.S. It's harder to see and you're more likely to be sharing the road with fellow drivers who are either tired and could potentially doze off or have had one too many drinks and are driving under the influence.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, WGN 9
